response.justification: Bogdan brings valuable geospatial expertise through founding GISCollective and strong understanding of high-performance distributed systems from his Dunnhumby role handling millions of requests per second. However, his C++ skills are severely outdated (last work 2014-2015) and his career has shifted toward TypeScript/web development over the past four years, moving away from systems programming. While geospatial knowledge is a strong domain fit, the gap in modern C++, lack of Rust experience, and absence of navigation systems background create substantial ramp-up risk for a role requiring modern C++ as primary technology.
strengths: Extensive geospatial domain expertise through founding GISCollective and building web GIS applications for mapping and data collection
Proven ability to design high-performance, low-latency systems from Dunnhumby role handling millions of requests/second with asynchronous I/O and caching
Demonstrated willingness to work across diverse technical stacks and learn new languages, as evidenced by proficiency in C++, DLang, JavaScript, Java, and Swift across various roles
concerns: C++ skills severely outdated with last relevant experience in December 2014; no modern C++ or any Rust development on record
Career trajectory over past four years focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and web/full-stack development rather than systems programming
No navigation systems or routing-specific experience; geospatial knowledge differs from navigation guidance solution requirements
